app.title: ChashApp
app.subtitle: Secure file and note encryption for Windows, Linux and macOS with clean UX, audit history and portfolio-ready architecture.
files.intro: Encrypt and decrypt multiple files in one pass. Outputs are saved next to the originals.
files.securityBody: ChashApp encrypts data with AES-256-GCM and derives the key from your password using the selected KDF. Security still depends on using a strong password and keeping backups.
notes.intro: Encrypt sensitive notes directly in the app and save the result to a file or clipboard.
notes.previewHint: Enter plain text or encrypted payload on the left, and review the result on the right.
history.intro: Track operations without storing passwords. Export the history to JSON or CSV.
common.kdfHint: PBKDF2 is widely compatible. Argon2id is a stronger modern choice for password hardening, but may be slower on older hardware.
common.actionNeedsPassword: Choose a file or enter a password to enable this action.
files.dropHint: Drop files or whole folders here. Folder contents will be added recursively.
history.empty: No history entries match the current filter yet.
settings.intro: Configure language, default KDF, privacy, and application behavior.
settings.secureDelete: Overwrite and delete source files after success
settings.privacyMode: Privacy mode: the app does not upload your data
settings.portableMode: Portable mode is enabled automatically when a `portable.mode` file is present next to the app.
settings.recoveryHint: Keep backups of important encrypted files. If the password is lost, the encrypted content is effectively unrecoverable.
settings.privacyHint: ChashApp works locally on this device. It does not upload files, notes, or passwords as part of its normal operation.
settings.releasePrepBody: Settings are stored locally, the default KDF is remembered, and the app is ready for self-contained publishing.
about.title: What is ChashApp?
about.body: ChashApp is a local desktop app for encrypting files, folders, and notes with a user-provided password. It is an offline encryption tool, not a cloud service, backup system, or password manager.
about.howBody: You provide your own password, the app derives an encryption key from it, and then encrypts the content. If the password is wrong or the encrypted payload was modified, decryption should fail.
about.privacyBody: Files and notes are processed locally, and history does not store passwords. Secure erase reduces simple recovery risk, but it should not be treated as an absolute guarantee on every disk or filesystem.
about.panicHint: Panic mode clears visible secrets and closes the app quickly, but it is a convenience feature, not a formal anti-forensics guarantee.
status.integrityOk: Encrypted payload structure and authentication check passed.
status.verifyRequiresEncrypted: Verify works only for .chash containers created by ChashApp.
status.secureEraseDone: Selected files were overwritten and removed.
status.secureDeleteDone: Source file was overwritten and removed.
status.autoLocked: Session auto-locked. Password fields were cleared.
dialog.uninstallNow: Run the ChashApp uninstaller now? The app will close before uninstall starts.
dialog.secureEraseNow: Overwrite and remove the selected files now? This action should be treated as irreversible.
close.title: Close application?
close.message: Are you sure you want to close ChashApp? Unsaved form data may be lost.
